dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9588 IH: Federal Responsibility and Accountability for Unchecked Dereliction Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Stauber (for himself, Mr. Emmer, Mr. Finstad, and Mrs. Fischbach) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

form.official-title: To require certain State officials to report fraud involving Federal funds, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Responsibility and Accountability for Unchecked Dereliction Act or the FRAUD Act.

section.header: Duty to report fraud involving Federal funds

subsection.text: Not later than 180 days after receiving a fraud warning involving covered Federal funds, a covered official shall report such warning to the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

subsection.header: Condition of receipt of Federal funds
subsection.text: In order to be eligible to receive Federal funds for a fiscal year, the chief executive of a State shall certify to the head of the Federal agency providing such funds that during the previous fiscal year the covered officials of the State have substantially complied with the requirement under subsection (a).
Whoever, being a covered official, knowingly—

paragraph.text: violates the requirement under subsection (a); or
obstructs, interferes with, or impedes a Federal, State, or Inspector General investigation involving fraud involving covered Federal funds,

subsection.continuation-text: shall be fined under title 18, United States Code, imprisoned for not more than 10 years, or both.

subsection.text: The head of an agency that oversees the distribution of Federal funds, with respect to which a covered official has been convicted of an offense under subsection (c), may prohibit such covered official from overseeing the distribution of such funds.

section.text: Nothing in this Act shall be construed to—

paragraph.text: authorize the Federal Government to remove a State official from elected office; or
violate principles of federalism under the Tenth Amendment to the Constitution of the United States.

subparagraph.text: the head of a State agency that administers, oversees, distributes, or certifies expenditures of covered Federal funds; and
any State Inspector General, or equivalent State official established by law, with statutory responsibility for investigating, auditing, or overseeing covered Federal funds.

paragraph.text: The term fraud warning means a written allegation of a specific instance of fraud involving covered Federal funds, supported by documentary evidence, which may include a written finding, a referral, an audit, an investigative report, or a whistleblower complaint, alleging the misuse, theft, diversion, or improper expenditure of not less than $250,000 in covered Federal funds.

The term covered Federal funds means any Federal funds provided to a State or State agency through a grant, cooperative agreement, reimbursement program, direct appropriation, or other Federal funding mechanism.
